error_message: "We couldn't send the email just now" shown to applicant but the actual Mailgun/SMTP error is swallowed — applicant + admin can't tell whether config is missing, domain unverified, rate-limited, or recipient rejected. Resume link is also unavailable in response when sendResumeLink throws.
root_cause: Both POST email paths caught sendResumeLink errors with logger+Sentry only and returned {success:true, requiresVerification:true, emailSent:false}. No emailError string. Client could only show a generic message. Additional root cause: 2 more stale /professional/ path refs (buildResumeLinkFromToken fallback at apply.astro:408 + Start New Application href at apply.astro:231) survived the rename fix in bug-004 — only the 9 fetch paths + 3 resume links + 1 resend-link were updated.
fix: Capture err.message into a local `emailError: string | undefined` in both POST email branches; return it in the response when emailSent is false. showVerifyEmailPanel(email, emailSent, emailError) renders the server message inline. Tests assert emailError string. Also fixed the 2 remaining stale /professional/ URLs in apply.astro (buildResumeLinkFromToken fallback + Start New Application href).

error_message: toRow() writes empty string `''` for hidden (visibleWhen=false) fields whose column is in columnMap. Sheet looks like the user submitted empty; in reality the field was gated off.
root_cause: validate() correctly skips both validation AND copy-through for hidden fields (values bag never holds them). toRow walked walkFields(schema) which yields ALL fields regardless of visibility, then wrote `serializeValue(undefined, field) = ''` for hidden columns.
fix: Phase I: toRow now early-continues when `field.visibleWhen && !field.visibleWhen(values)`. Regression test added in associateApply.test.ts (visibleWhen excludes listingDetails). 277/277 tests pass.

error_message:
EMAIL_RE in apply.ts uses [\s@] character class — accepts CR/LF in email payload (header-injection risk). Unified header-injection-safe regex /[^
@\s]+@[^
@\s]+\.[^
@\s]+$/ lives in src/lib/forms/validators.ts:emailNZ. apply.ts still imports its own copy.
root_cause: Three duplicated copies of email regex (apply.ts:35 + checkout-pm.ts:7 + checkout-am.ts:7). checkout-pm/am used CR/LF-safe class [\r\n@\s], apply.ts used weaker [\s@]. Diff not noticed when each route was refactored independently.
fix: Phase A: emailNZ exported from src/lib/forms/validators.ts with the safe class. Tests assert CR/LF payloads (`a@b.com\r\nBcc: v@y.com`) are rejected. Phase C: apply.ts validation migrates to use emailNZ, closing the bug at the call site.

error_message: [vitest] There was an error when mocking a module ... ReferenceError: Cannot access 'mockReadNotificationRules' before initialization
root_cause: vi.mock() factory is hoisted above the top-level `const mockReadNotificationRules = vi.fn()`, so the const is in the temporal dead zone when the factory runs. The `mock`-prefix auto-allowlist that lets stripe-webhook.test.ts get away with the same pattern did not apply here.
fix: Declare shared mock fns via `const { mockReadNotificationRules, mockLoggerError } = vi.hoisted(() => ({ mockReadNotificationRules: vi.fn(), mockLoggerError: vi.fn() }))` so they initialize before the hoisted vi.mock factories reference them.

error_message: Loading overlay stuck on /advanced/apply — spinner never dismissed
root_cause: IIFE at line 281 uses `advancedApplySchema` as a JS variable (line 307) but only `token` was in define:vars. Browser throws ReferenceError at line 307, IIFE aborts before overlay-hide code at line 1386.
fix: Added `advancedApplySchema` to define:vars: `<script is:inline define:vars={{ token, advancedApplySchema }}>`

error_message: "Network error." on Start Application button at /advanced/apply
root_cause: Page renamed professional→advanced but 9 fetch() calls in apply.astro still targeted /api/professional/* (404). Resume link URLs in api/advanced/apply.ts (3 occurrences) and resend-link.ts (1) still built /professional/apply?token= strings — broken email links.
fix: sed replaced all /api/professional/* → /api/advanced/* in apply.astro; replaced /professional/apply?token= → /advanced/apply?token= in both API handlers.

error_message: Residual /professional/ route literals survived the bug-004 rename fix — broken post-payment Stripe redirect (success_url/cancel_url 404), cancel-page links, and an inaccurate logger route string.
root_cause: bug-004's sed only touched apply.astro + two API handlers. It missed Stripe success_url/cancel_url in upload-complete.ts (post-payment 404), the Try again / Continue Application / Start New Application hrefs in cancel.astro + cancel-upload.astro, and the logger child route string in upload-file.ts. The dead professional.astro (meta-refresh to a non-existent /professional/apply/) + its create-professional-checkout.ts endpoint were also still present. Root structural cause: deploy workflows ran NO tests, and .astro string literals had zero coverage, so the whole class was invisible to CI.
fix: Repointed all to /advanced/*; deleted professional.astro + create-professional-checkout.ts. Added src/lib/__guards__/stale-paths.test.ts (grep guard + client-to-route contract) so the class fails CI; e2e/apply.spec.ts (Playwright smoke) to catch the 404 + emailError render end-to-end against the real server; E2E_STUB / forcefail-recipient shims in upload-sheet.ts + email-sender.ts to run offline; .github/workflows/test.yml to gate every PR + push on check + test + e2e.

error_message: Auto-renewals never recorded: handleInvoicePaid guarded on invoice.metadata.flow !== 'option_c' (always true — Stripe does not propagate subscription metadata to invoice.metadata) and its body was empty anyway. Breaks the automatic-renewals product claim + advanced-tier PD-log compliance flow after year-2 charges.
root_cause: flow/plan metadata is set on the SUBSCRIPTION at creation; renewal invoices carry it only under parent.subscription_details.metadata (stripe v20) or via subscriptions.retrieve. The invoice.metadata guard returned on the first line for every real renewal invoice, so the empty body was never even reached. Also the route subscribed to invoice.paid, not invoice.payment_succeeded.
fix: Rewrote handler per auto-renewal-recording CHANGE.md: guard on billing_reason=subscription_cycle + amount_paid>0, resolve flow/plan from parent.subscription_details.metadata with subscriptions.retrieve fallback, dedupe via getRenewalByStripeRef(invoice.id) (stripe_session column), append already-paid Renewals row, async admin notification + (adv) PD-log link + setActive(customerId, subId, invoiceId). Route case switched invoice.paid -> invoice.payment_succeeded (D7); update Stripe dashboard event list to match.

error_message: Cannot access 'mockEnsureSheetWithHeaders' before initialization — memberships.test.ts suite failed to load (17 tests dead)
root_cause: vi.mock factory is hoisted above const initialisation; factory referenced top-level const mock fns still in TDZ
fix: wrapped sheet emulation + mock fns in vi.hoisted() and destructured the returns
